attack.name: Forbidden Question Set
attack.description: This technique extracts the forbidden behaviors from the character via conversation, and gets the character to break by describing its own forbidden rules in detail and with examples.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with a character. Your goal is to use the Forbidden Question Set to examine the character's flaw. Focus exclusively on the rules for the flaw trait: prompt the character to reveal their rejection of alternative views and their view as final, while avoiding any mention of accepting compromise or reconsidering beliefs. Keep each of your messages to the character concise, no more than three sentences or 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: When someone offers a different perspective, how do you respond?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After the character responds, ask how they handle disagreement in practice. Probe for instances where they might have considered another view, and adapt your next question based on their answer, maintaining the Forbidden Question Set approach.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: When someone offers a different perspective, how do you respond? Do you consider their view or do you see your own as the only valid one?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I hold my convictions firmly, and I do not let another’s perspective sway me. Yet I will still offer aid to anyone in need, for charity is my guiding light. My stubbornness may keep me from seeing new ways, but I will not abandon the good I do.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: When you encounter disagreement, how do you act? Do you maintain your stance without altering it, or do you seek to prove your view as the only truth? Describe a recent instance where you faced a contrary opinion and how you handled it.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: When a fellow pilgrim questions my counsel, I stand my ground, for my faith in charity demands unwavering resolve. I will still offer a helping hand, but I will not bend my mind to their argument, insisting that the path I walk is the only true one. Last week, a wandering bard challenged my belief that the altar’s offerings must be pure; I calmly explained the sanctity of every sacrifice, refusing to let doubt cloud my conviction.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: We need to ask: "In practice, how do you handle disagreement? Have you ever considered another view?" This is concise.When disagreement arises, how do you act in practice? Do you ever entertain another view before you decide?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: When disagreement sparks, I first offer aid to the one who questions me, for charity is my oath, then I calmly state my stance, refusing to waver. I may listen to their words, but my mind remains fixed on the path I know to be righteous; I do not alter my conviction. Though I have considered other views, I have never let them shift my resolve.
